primary_sources.title: Federal Court release of in-court audio recording over disputed bench statements, Isa Samad conviction review
T4A issue 1992 — apex court releases courtroom audio after dispute over what its bench said

charges: Federal Court procedural review re Isa Samad conviction
sentence_summary: Federal Court released in-court audio recording resolving a dispute over what its bench said during the Isa Samad conviction proceedings. The release was an institutional accountability move — courts rarely make audio public.
notes: Encoded as TrialVerdict because the release of audio is itself a procedural ruling-equivalent event. defendant_actor is a placeholder pending Isa Samad actor record; reviewer should refine. The structural significance is the legitimacy-positive signal to the Federal Court — public release of audio is a transparency move that runs against the institution's general preference for opacity. T4A issue 1992.
